title: Sequential Agent Pipeline
constraints: The researcher agent must run before the writer agent
The writer must receive the researcher's output as input
You may not merge both roles into a single agent
The pipeline must run end-to-end with a single invocation
hints: Capture the researcher's output and pass it as context to the writer
In CrewAI, use a sequential Process so tasks execute in order with automatic context passing
In LangChain, chain the two agents using RunnableSequence or call them sequentially in code

starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

researcher = Agent(
    role="Researcher",
    goal="Gather key facts about a given topic",
    backstory="You are a thorough research analyst.",
    llm=llm,
)

writer = Agent(
    role="Writer",
    goal="Write a concise summary from research notes",
    backstory="You are a skilled technical writer.",
    llm=llm,
)

research_task = Task(
    description="Research the latest trends in {topic}",
    expected_output="A list of 5 key findings with brief explanations",
    agent=researcher,
)

write_task = Task(
    description="Write a 200-word summary about {topic}",
    expected_output="A polished summary paragraph",
    agent=writer,
)

# BUG: Both tasks run independently — the writer never sees the research
# TODO: Wire the tasks so the writer receives the researcher's output
crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, writer],
    tasks=[research_task, write_task],
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"topic": "AI agents"})
print(result)
starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

research_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a thorough research analyst. Gather key facts about the given topic."),
    ("human", "Research the latest trends in {topic}"),
])

write_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a skilled technical writer. Write a concise summary."),
    ("human", "Write a 200-word summary about {topic}"),
])

research_chain = research_prompt | llm
write_chain = write_prompt | llm

# BUG: Both chains run independently — the writer never sees the research
# TODO: Feed the researcher's output into the writer's input
research_result = research_chain.invoke({"topic": "AI agents"})
print("Research:", research_result.content)

write_result = write_chain.invoke({"topic": "AI agents"})
print("Summary:", write_result.content)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

researcher = Agent(
    name="Researcher",
    instructions="You are a thorough research analyst. Gather key facts about the given topic.",
)

writer = Agent(
    name="Writer",
    instructions="You are a skilled technical writer. Write a concise summary.",
)

# BUG: Both agents run independently — the writer never sees the research
# TODO: Feed the researcher's output into the writer's input
research_result = Runner.run_sync(researcher, "Research the latest trends in AI agents")
print("Research:", research_result.final_output)

write_result = Runner.run_sync(writer, "Write a 200-word summary about AI agents")
print("Summary:", write_result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Sequential execution order
evaluationCriteria.description: The researcher runs first and the writer runs second

evaluationCriteria.label: Research context flows to writer
evaluationCriteria.description: The writer's input includes the researcher's output so the summary is grounded in actual research

evaluationCriteria.label: Coherent final summary
evaluationCriteria.description: The final summary clearly reflects the specific findings from the research step
